name: real_init_vacuous_none
code:
proof fn phi_1_real_init_vacuous_none(netc: &NetClient, args: Args)
    requires
        netc.valid(),
    ensures
        HostState::init_ensures(netc, args, None),
{
}
reason: If init_ensures is trivially satisfied by None, the implementation can always skip initialization

name: init_nonempty_h
code:
proof fn phi_2_init_nonempty_h(pre: AbstractHostState, id: AbstractEndPoint, args: AbstractArgs)
    requires
        init(pre, id, args),
    ensures
        pre.h.dom().len() > 0,
{
}
reason: A freshly initialized host should have an empty hash table; a non-empty one would imply phantom state

name: init_zero_delegations
code:
proof fn phi_3_init_zero_delegations(pre: AbstractHostState, id: AbstractEndPoint, args: AbstractArgs)
    requires
        init(pre, id, args),
    ensures
        pre.num_delegations == 0,
{
}
reason: Init sets num_delegations to 1; allowing 0 would break delegation-count tracking invariants

name: parse_args_always_none
reason: If parse_args never succeeds, no host can ever be initialized, making the entire system vacuously correct

name: to_vec_always_empty
reason: An always-empty spec_to_vec means the hash map can never faithfully report its stored key-value pairs
